feed.title: Combined summary - RFC: Two years of BIP Editing, time for some new faces?

entry.title: Combined summary - RFC: Two years of BIP Editing, time for some new faces?
entry.summary: Over the past two years, significant progress has been made in the management and operation of the Bitcoin Improvement Proposals (BIPs) repository. A team of BIP Editors, initially expanded two years ago, has actively engaged in enhancing the protocol's documentation and processes. This period saw the processing of 596 pull requests, with 382 successfully merged, and the publication of 42 new BIPs. These efforts were complemented by updates to existing documents, clean-ups of abandoned submissions, and improvements to continuous integration scripts. The repository experienced a noticeable increase in activity and submissions, indicating a revitalized interest and engagement from the community. The workload during this time was predominantly handled by two active editors, with one taking on a significantly larger portion of daily tasks in the recent quarter. This situation underlines the necessity for a well-staffed editorial team to manage the volume and complexity of the work effectively. In response to the evolving demands and to sustain the momentum, there is a proposal to refresh the BIP Editor team by selecting three dedicated individuals who are committed to regular contribution. This approach aims to balance the workload more evenly and maintain the high standards of oversight and facilitation needed for the BIPs repository. For those interested in becoming BIP Editors, it is important to have some experience with Bitcoin projects or protocol design, a solid command of English, and an aptitude for detail-oriented tasks. The role requires a weekly commitment of 5-10 hours, with responsibilities that include monitoring open pull requests, encouraging timely reviews and updates, merging ready pull requests, and providing constructive feedback on both new and existing BIPs. Most of the editorial work, except for specific tasks like labeling and merging pull requests, can be performed by any community member, which provides a good opportunity for prospective editors to demonstrate their capabilities and readiness for the role by engaging with current open pull requests available at [the BIPs GitHub page](https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/pulls?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Apr+-label%3A%22Metadata+Update%22+-label%3ATypo-to-be-collected+-label%3ACI). This initiative not only reflects the ongoing dedication to improving the Bitcoin infrastructure but also emphasizes the collaborative effort required to manage such a dynamic and critical component of the Bitcoin ecosystem. The community's continued participation and support are crucial in driving forward these improvements and ensuring the robustness and reliability of future BIP processes.
